title: Rental income and non-resident tax - Canada.ca
content:
## Rental income and non-resident tax
Find out your filing and reporting requirements if you are a non-resident who received rental income from real and immovable property in Canada.
- Filing and reporting requirements
- Important reminder about Form NR6 , Undertaking to File an Income Tax Return by a Non-Resident Receiving Rent from Real Property or Receiving a Timber Royalty
- Subsection 216(1) Late-filing Policy

## Forms and publications
- Guide T4144 , Income Tax Guide for Electing under Section 216
- Form NR6 , Undertaking to File an Income Tax Return by a Non-Resident Receiving Rent from Real or Immovable Property or Receiving a Timber Royalty
- Form T1159 , Income Tax Return for Electing under Section 216
- Non-residents of Canada
- Lines 12599 and 12600 – Rental income

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
